content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN integers. You need to output the **maximum possible sum** of any contiguous subarray of AAA containing **non-negative** numbers only.

Note: A contiguous subarray is obtained by deletion of several (possibly zero) elements from the beginning of the array and several (possibly zero) elements from the end of the array. If there is no sub-array containing only non-negative numbers, then print 000.

Note: The answer may be larger than 323232 bits.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN — the length of array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers - the elements of array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible sum of any contiguous subarray of AAA containing non-negative numbers only.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 200000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the maximum possible sum of any contiguous subarray that consists only of non‑negative numbers. If no such subarray exists, output 0.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: the whole array is non‑negative, sum = 15.
Test case 2: the best non‑negative subarray is [10], sum = 10.
Test case 3: the best subarray is [10,0,8], sum = 18.
Test case 4: no non‑negative element, answer = 0.
All elements are zero, the whole array is a valid subarray with sum 0.
The maximal non‑negative contiguous segment is [5,5] with sum 10.

hidden.label: edge case with zeros and negatives

solution.approach: Traverse the array once while maintaining the sum of the current segment of consecutive non‑negative numbers. Whenever a negative number is encountered, reset the current sum to 0. After processing each element, update the answer with the maximum of the current sum and the best answer seen so far. This yields the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray containing only non‑negative numbers. If the array has no non‑negative element, the answer remains 0.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the array.
